title: PermitApplication
description: NET-NEW transactional write object: an API-first DOB NOW filing. Today a permit application (a DOB NOW: Build job filing) can only be submitted through the browser-only DOB NOW web application (a810-dobnow.nyc.gov); there is no public API to file, pay for, or check the status of one — the only machine-readable output is a nightly Socrata dump of already-processed filings. This schema models the applicant-submitted filing as an owned, agent-native resource so that an architect, expediter, or homeowner (or their agent) can create, submit, and track a filing programmatically. It is the write mirror of the read-only JobFiling/Permit records.

x-source.app: https://a810-dobnow.nyc.gov (DOB NOW: Build) — browser-only filing, no API
x-source.note: There is no existing write API. This is the proposed net-new transactional surface for the DOB domain.

applicationId.description: Server-assigned id for the in-progress application (read-only).

jobFilingNumber.description: DOB NOW Job Filing Number, assigned once the filing is created (read-only).

status.description: Lifecycle state of the application (read-only; advanced by DOB review).

jobType.description: Filing type, e.g. A1, A2, A3, NB, DM.

workTypes.description: Trades/work permits requested (e.g. GC, PL, MH, EW, BL, SP, FS, FA).

professionalCert.description: Whether the filing is professionally self-certified.

bin.description: Building Identification Number the work is on.

attachments.description: Supporting documents (plans, PW1, technical reports) as references.
